symbols: red banner falling from sky — claimed divine provision, a sign from heaven granting victory
cross on banner/standard — the sign of Christ under which the crusaders fought
bishop in white vestments — the spiritual authority blessing and directing the battle
sword raised — martial conflict and the call to arms
stormy sky with break in clouds — divine intervention breaking through earthly chaos
tree at right edge — a grounding in the earthly landscape amid heavenly signs
description: A dramatic battlefield scene. At center, armored warriors in medieval gear look upward and point toward a red banner or cloth falling from a bright break in the stormy clouds above. A figure in white ecclesiastical vestments stands among them, also gesturing upward. One soldier raises a sword. To the left, more troops engage in distant combat with horses and raised weapons amid smoke and dust. The sky is dark and turbulent with a luminous opening where the banner descends. At right, a tree and a cross-topped standard mark the foreground.

_meta.title: Dannebrog Falling from the Sky During the Battle of Lyndanisse, June 15, 1219
